LHX.sentence: L3Harris is trading at its highest premium to other defense stocks in years, which suggests the market might be getting ahead of itself despite the company's solid fundamentals.

ASML.sentence: ASML is up about 2.4% today and looks like it might get cheaper relative to earnings over the next year as business growth catches up to the stock price.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet day with barely any movement — the industrial gas business just keeps grinding along steadily.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft dipped slightly today, but all eyes are on the upcoming quarterly results to see if Azure cloud growth stays above 30%, which would validate the AI investment story.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is down a bit and looks attractively priced right now — the lab equipment business is at a cyclical low point, so the stock is trading cheaper than usual.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is up slightly today and its dividend yield is unusually close to Treasury rates, though that gap should widen as interest rates come down.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow gained about 0.7% today and is still down more than half from its peak, making it one of the cheaper ways to play the enterprise AI automation trend.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore is up nearly 2% today and outperforming other copper miners, benefiting from both its mining operations and its commodity trading business.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin dropped about 1.2% today and is sitting about 16% below its long-term trend line, which historically has been a decent entry point.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA is flat today — the Mexican peso exchange rate is stable, which is usually the main thing that moves this stock around.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras dropped 1.5% today despite paying an enormous 61% dividend yield, which either means oil prices are unsustainably high or Brazilian politics are weighing on the stock.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk is up 2.4% today but still trading below the actual value of its ships, suggesting the market thinks global shipping demand might weaken.
